title: Novavax Q4 2023 Financial Results: Transition Year with Progress Towards Strengthening Financial Profile
text: Novavax reported fourth-quarter 2023 revenues of $291 million, down from $357 million in the same period of 2022. For the full year, the company generated $984 million in revenue compared to $2 billion in 2022. The company posted a net loss of $178 million for Q4 2023, compared to a $182 million loss in Q4 2022. Full-year net loss reached $545 million versus $658 million in 2022. Operating expenses dropped significantly by 41% year-over-year. CEO John Jacobs stated: 2023 was a transition year for Novavax and the company has made tremendous progress towards strengthening the financial profile of the Company. The company achieved a 30% workforce reduction and exceeded cost-reduction targets by approximately $150 million. Management provided guidance of $800 million to $1 billion in total revenue for 2024, with combined research and development and selling, general, and administrative expenses projected at $700-$800 million. Novavax expects to initiate a Phase 3 trial for a COVID-19-influenza combination vaccine in the second half of 2024, with a potential 2026 launch anticipated.
